title: Smoking status cardiac review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

assessment.text: Supports current and former tobacco status, product use, willingness and readiness to quit, previous attempts, motivation, confidence, barriers, and dependence assessment including smoking rate and time to first cigarette.
assessment.rationale: The UAE MOHAP guideline directly supports documenting current or recent tobacco use, product and quantity context, dependence-related questions, prior attempts, and readiness when discussed; it does not assume smoking or generate cessation advice.
assessment.evidence_extract: Supports current and former tobacco status, product use, willingness and readiness to quit, previous attempts, motivation, confidence, barriers, and dependence assessment including smoking rate and time to first cigarette.

source.title: Practical Guideline for Management of Tobacco Dependence

exact_section.heading: The 5 As and Identification and Assessment of Tobacco Use Status

treatment_and_management.text: Supports clinician-led cardiovascular risk review and documented lifestyle discussion without autonomously generating advice.
treatment_and_management.rationale: NG238 supports recording smoking as one clinician-assessed cardiovascular-risk factor, patient preferences, and an entered plan without calculating risk or prescribing an intervention.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-led cardiovascular risk review and documented lifestyle discussion without autonomously generating advice.

source.title: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.3.12 — cardiovascular risk and lifestyle review

source_limitations: Current status, product, amount, duration, dependence, previous attempts, relapse, exposure, preferences, readiness, cardiovascular risk calculation, counseling content, treatment, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
